paper_title: DFT study of methane catalytic combustion on Pd-based diatomic catalysts

methods.description: Process for optimizing the structures of reactants, products, intermediates, and transition states using DFT calculations, and performing frequency analysis to confirm the nature of these structures.

steps.description: The structural towards all reactants, products, intermediates and transition states (TSs) are initially optimized at the B3LYP/6-311G(d, p)+LANL2DZ level.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize molecular structures using B3LYP/6-311G(d, p)+LANL2DZ

steps.description: The optimized structures of reactants, products and intermediates are no imaginary frequency, whereas TSs are only one imaginary vibration frequency.
steps.searchable_summary: perform frequency analysis to confirm structure nature

methods.description: Perform single point energy calculations on optimized structures to obtain more accurate energy values.

steps.description: All the single point calculations are performed with B3LYP/6-311++G(d, p)+SDD level.
steps.searchable_summary: perform single point energy calculations using B3LYP/6-311++G(d, p)+SDD

methods.description: Calculate the intrinsic reaction coordinate to confirm that the optimized transition states correctly connect the relevant reactants and products.

steps.description: The calculations of intrinsic reaction coordinate (IRC) are made to confirm that the optimized TSs correctly connect the relevant reactants and products.
steps.searchable_summary: perform IRC calculations to verify transition states

methods.description: Calculate the activation energy using transition state theory, considering the reaction temperature and other thermodynamic parameters.

steps.searchable_summary: calculate activation energy using transition state theory
